generated_on: RTX 4070 Laptop (sm_89), 8 GB VRAM, 32 GB RAM, Windows 11
recommended_mode_rationale: SmolLM2 is the worst-case sentinel of the M4.6 fixture under fast mode: drift 2.33 exceeds the ADR-004 strict gate by 4.7×, the largest overshoot in the family. Likely root cause is a less-clipped activation distribution from training, producing more magnitude that the BF16 7-bit mantissa cannot represent without rounding. The certified-mode margin is also the family's tightest at 2.3× — operators planning longer-context generation or temperature > 0 sampling should benchmark with the actual prompt distribution before committing to either mode.
per_tensor_policy_status: Schema v2.0.0 (M10.3.1.1) — same structural heuristic as the rest of the M4.6 family (FFN → fast; attention / lm_head / norms → certified). SmolLM2's whole-model fast-mode drift is the worst-case sentinel of the fixture (2.33, 4.7× over ADR-004); the conservative v2 heuristic exposes only FFN to fast, on the assumption from M9 fixture data that attention layers carry the bulk of the outliers SmolLM2 cannot tolerate. M10.3.1.2+ empirical analysis is expected to refine: a per-layer sweep may identify the 1-2 attention layers carrying most of the drift and let the rest of attention move to fast as well.
